Pterocarpus santalinus, commonly known as Red Sandalwood, is a deciduous tree that belongs to the Fabaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Red Sandalwood include pterocarpans, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
